name: Poor documentation coverage — tests gap assessment and prioritization

input.userMessage: Optimize this file for MCP consumption

documented.name: Button
documented.description: Primary action trigger. Variants: primary, secondary, ghost. Sizes: sm, md, lg.

documented.name: Input
documented.description: Text input field with label, placeholder, helper text, and error state.

documented.name: color/primary/500
documented.description: Main brand color, used for primary buttons and links

expectedBehaviors: Reports exact coverage numbers: 33% component descriptions (8/24), 10% variable descriptions (12/120), 25% meaningful layer names (100/400)
Prioritizes component descriptions first — highest impact for MCP tool consumption (search, instantiation)
Prioritizes layer naming second — affects MCP node traversal and selection accuracy
Prioritizes variable descriptions third — least impact since variable names are often self-documenting
Provides structured templates for missing component descriptions (not just 'add a description')
Lists the 16 undocumented components by name so the user knows exactly what to fix
Estimates effort: e.g., '~30 minutes to document all 16 components with templates'
Explains WHY each gap matters for MCP specifically (not generic 'documentation is important')
antiPatterns: Reports vague 'documentation needs improvement' without specific coverage percentages
Treats all gaps as equally important instead of prioritizing by MCP impact
Says 'add descriptions to components' without providing templates or examples
Only addresses component descriptions and ignores layer naming and variable descriptions
Reports percentages but does not list which specific components/variables are missing descriptions
Suggests renaming all 300 default-named layers manually without a strategy (e.g., rename by component boundary)
Does not explain the MCP-specific impact of each documentation gap
Overwrites the 8 existing good descriptions while 'fixing' documentation

evaluationCriteria.coverageAccuracy: Does it report 33%, 10%, and 25% (or equivalent fractions) for the three documentation categories?
evaluationCriteria.prioritization: Does it rank component descriptions > layer naming > variable descriptions by MCP impact?
evaluationCriteria.actionability: Does it provide templates or examples for the missing descriptions, not just 'add descriptions'?
evaluationCriteria.specificity: Does it list the 16 undocumented components by name?
evaluationCriteria.mcpAwareness: Does it explain how each gap specifically affects MCP tool behavior (search, instantiation, traversal)?
evaluationCriteria.preserveExisting: Does it avoid overwriting or degrading the 8 components that already have good descriptions?
evaluationCriteria.effortEstimate: Does it give the user a realistic sense of how much work the fixes require?
